title: The jar that won't start on the lab machine

mcq.question_md: You run `mvn package`, copy the jar to the lab machine, and run `java -jar studentportal.jar`. It fails with `no main manifest attribute`. What's going on?
options: The lab machine has the wrong Java version installed
The plain jar doesn't record which class holds main(); the manifest needs to name it (e.g. via the Shade plugin)
Jars built by Maven can only be run with the mvn command
You must rename the jar to match your main class
mcq.explanation_md: A default Maven jar contains your compiled classes but its manifest doesn't say **which class has `main()`** — so `java -jar` has no idea where to start. Configuring the main class in the manifest (the Shade plugin does this, and also bundles your dependencies into a fat jar) fixes it. The tempting wrong answer is the Java version: a version mismatch gives a *different* error (`UnsupportedClassVersionError`), not this one. And jars are plain `java -jar` runnable anywhere — needing Maven at *run* time would defeat the point of packaging.
